rationale: The bright blue/high-energy palette and young-male anime audience have some surface overlap with Archery Clash!'s competitive PvP demo, but a Goku Ultra Instinct charge-up has zero connection to aiming/shooting/dueling mechanics and would mislead installs into a bait-and-switch experience. Major frictions: IP/legal risk using Goku likeness, no archery or trajectory hook, and the install intent skews toward anime ARPG fans rather than skill-shot duelers. Skip unless reworked into an original anime-styled archer character showcase that retains the rainbow VFX energy but swaps the charge-sphere for a bow-draw payoff.
